name: sqlx (Rust)
what_it_does: Async-first pure Rust SQL library with compile-time query verification. sqlx uses Rust macros (query!, query_as!) to verify SQL syntax and types at compile time against a live database — misspelled column names or wrong types are compiler errors. Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite with full async support on Tokio. No ORM — you write SQL; sqlx validates and maps results.
use_cases: Write raw SQL queries in async Rust services with compile-time type checking against actual database schema
Map SQL results directly to Rust structs using query_as! with automatic row-to-struct conversion
Use with Axum or Actix-web for async web services requiring high-performance database access
Run database migrations with sqlx-migrate (sqlx migrate run) integrated into the build/deploy pipeline
Achieve maximum SQL performance in Rust services without ORM overhead while keeping compile-time safety
not_for: Teams wanting ORM features (relationships, migrations as code, active record) — use SeaORM or Diesel for ORM patterns
Non-async applications — sqlx is async-first; use Diesel for synchronous database access
Databases other than PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite — sqlx doesn't support MSSQL or Oracle
best_when: You want to write SQL in async Rust with compile-time query verification and type-safe row mapping without an ORM.
avoid_when: You need ORM features (relationships, active record patterns) — use SeaORM or Diesel.

auth.notes: Library with no auth — database credentials in connection URL.

pricing.notes: Free and open source, maintained by LaunchBadge.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — local library. Pure async Rust with connection pooling via sqlx::Pool.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: sqlx provides compile-time errors for invalid SQL or type mismatches via macros. Runtime errors are sqlx::Error variants with descriptive messages. Database errors include the SQL error code and message.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Queries are idempotent reads. Writes are developer responsibility.
known_agent_gotchas: compile-time query checking with query! macro requires DATABASE_URL environment variable set at build time — CI/CD must have database access during build or use offline mode
Offline mode (sqlx prepare) generates query metadata files that must be committed to git — missing these files cause 'set DATABASE_URL' errors in offline CI environments
query_as! maps by column name by default — struct field names must match SQL column names (or use AS aliases); mismatches cause compile errors
sqlx::Pool has a default max_connections of 10 — under high load this may be insufficient; configure with PgPoolOptions::new().max_connections(n)
Nullable database columns map to Option<T> in Rust — forgetting Option for nullable columns causes compile-time type errors that mention type mismatch with expected
sqlx 0.7 removed the FromRow derive macro for structs with fields not in SQL result — all struct fields must be present in the SELECT query or use #[sqlx(skip)] attribute

security_score_components.security_notes: Parameterized queries prevent SQL injection. Memory safety from Rust eliminates buffer overflows. Compile-time query verification prevents runtime SQL errors. DATABASE_URL must be kept secret in env vars.
